faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Get answers to the most common questions about ClaimSaver+

legal.question: What's the difference between ClaimSaver+ and an attorney?
legal.answer: ClaimSaver+ is software: guided forms, secure storage, reminders, and education so you can prepare and submit your Florida PIP claim materials. We are not a law firm and do not provide legal advice, evaluate your claim’s merits, negotiate with insurers, or represent you. An attorney can advise on strategy, liability, coverage disputes, and represent you when your situation requires it. For advice about your specific matter, consult a licensed Florida attorney.

accidents.question: What types of accidents does ClaimSaver+ handle?
accidents.answer: The platform is designed for Florida motor vehicle-related PIP claims—including car, motorcycle, truck, pedestrian, and bicycle incidents—so you can document and pursue no-fault benefits you are entitled to seek under your policy. We do not guarantee outcomes or act as your representative.

documents.question: Do I need to provide documents for my claim?
documents.answer: Yes, you'll need to provide relevant documents such as police reports, medical records, insurance information, photos of the accident scene, and any other evidence related to your case. Our secure platform makes it easy to upload and organize these documents.

states.question: Is ClaimSaver+ available in all states?
states.answer: ClaimSaver+ is focused on Florida motor vehicle no-fault (PIP) claims. Other states may be added over time. We do not provide legal advice; for questions about your jurisdiction, consult a licensed attorney.

optionalServices.title: Platform access and optional notarization
optionalServices.subtitle: Guided PIP filing is our core product. Online notarization is a separate add-on—priced and purchased on its own when you need it.
optionalServices.pipBody: Access to guided PIP forms, secure document storage, tracking, and reminders. This is what you use to prepare and file your Florida no-fault claim.
optionalServices.notarizationBody: Remote document notarization via DocuSign. Add it at checkout if you need it, or review the service first—it is not part of the guided PIP form workflow.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Your claim. Your control. Our tools.
howItWorks.intro: You handle every step. We provide the platform that makes each step easier.
howItWorks.step1Title: Create your account & pay for access
howItWorks.step1Body: Sign in, complete checkout for platform access, and unlock the guided workflows and document tools.
howItWorks.step2Body: Walk through the PIP form one section at a time with plain-language prompts. Built-in validation catches formatting errors and missing fields before you submit.
howItWorks.step3Body: Upload police reports, medical records, photos, and insurance documents to your encrypted vault. Everything stays organized and accessible when you need it.
howItWorks.step4Body: Use calendars, reminders, and expense tracking—including awareness of common deadlines like Florida’s 14-day initial treatment rule (general information, not legal advice).
howItWorks.step5Title: Compile and submit your claim package
howItWorks.step5Body: When you’re ready, pull your documents together into a complete claim package and submit it to your insurer.
howItWorks.notarizationNote: Need a document notarized? We offer optional online notarization via DocuSign as a separate service from guided PIP filing—about $25 when you add it at checkout. It is not required to use the claim platform.

needProfessionalHelp.title: When Your Claim Needs Legal Expertise
needProfessionalHelp.subtitle: Some claims go beyond standard PIP filing. Here’s how to know if yours is one of them.
needProfessionalHelp.body: ClaimSaver+ is designed for standard Florida PIP claims—the kind that make up the vast majority of no-fault filings. But some situations involve complexities that go beyond form filing and documentation. Here are signals that your claim may benefit from a licensed attorney:

bullets.serious: Serious injury or stepping outside no-fault
bullets.advice: Any question that requires legal advice about your specific situation

needProfessionalHelp.closing: As we grow, we may add optional professional services as a separate offering—always clearly distinguished from standard platform access, with their own agreements and disclosures.

licenseCredentials.title: License & Credentials
licenseCredentials.subtitle: Transparency about what ClaimSaver+ is—and is not
licenseCredentials.body: The ClaimSaver+ product is guided claim software. It is not a law firm, not your representative with an insurance company, and not a substitute for legal or adjusting advice when your situation requires it.
licenseCredentials.optionalPA: As we grow, optional licensed public adjusting or other professional services may be offered as a separate product—with separate agreements and disclosures, not part of the standard $500 platform access.
licenseCredentials.verify: Verification links to the Florida Department of Financial Services (DFS) license lookup will be posted here for any credentials we publish.
licenseCredentials.staffLicenseBody: Staff adjuster and related Florida license numbers will appear here for public verification as soon as they are published for display. If you need confirmation before then, contact us and we will point you to the appropriate DFS records.
licenseCredentials.staffLicensePending: Public adjuster (3-20) or other credentials, if offered as a separate service later, will be listed separately with full disclosures.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Platform & technical support
contact.body: For help signing in, billing, or using the website, email us. We cannot answer questions about the legal merits of your specific claim or tell you what you should do in your case—those questions belong to a licensed attorney.

whatIs.question: What is digital notarization?
whatIs.answer: Digital notarization is a secure, online process where documents are notarized remotely using advanced technology and video conferencing, making it convenient and legally binding.

legal.question: Is digital notarization legally valid?
legal.answer: Yes, digital notarization is legally valid in all 50 states and is recognized by courts, government agencies, and businesses nationwide.

documents.question: What documents can be notarized online?
documents.answer: Most documents can be notarized online, including real estate documents, legal contracts, power of attorney forms, wills, business agreements, and more.

duration.question: How long does the process take?
duration.answer: The entire digital notarization process typically takes 5-10 minutes from start to finish, including document upload and notary verification.

requirements.question: What do I need to get started?
requirements.answer: You'll need a valid government-issued ID, a computer or mobile device with camera and microphone, and the document you want to notarize in PDF format.

security.question: How secure is the process?
security.answer: Our platform uses bank-level encryption and security measures. All documents are encrypted in transit and at rest, and we maintain complete audit trails for all transactions.

footer.description: Guided PIP claim filing for Florida drivers—secure documents, smart tools, flat pricing.
footer.legalText: ClaimSaver+ is a guided claim technology platform—not a law firm. We do not provide legal advice, legal representation, claim evaluation, or negotiation with insurers on your behalf. Information on this site is general and educational only. You file your no-fault (PIP) claim using our tools—you are the filer, not ClaimSaver+. For advice about your specific situation, consult a licensed attorney. We do not guarantee any outcome. Optional attorney or other professional referrals may be offered separately where permitted; they are not part of the standard platform fee.
footer.optionalNotarization: Optional online notarization (DocuSign) is available separately—see Notarization and Pricing.

hero.title: Tools for
hero.subtitle: Your Florida PIP Claim
hero.description: We built ClaimSaver+ so Florida drivers can file and manage no-fault claims with clear, guided tools—without paying contingency fees for paperwork you can complete with the platform.

about.title: About ClaimSaver+
about.paragraph1: ClaimSaver+ is a guided claim filing platform for Florida motor vehicle no-fault (PIP) claims. You enter your information, upload your documents, and submit your claim materials. We provide software, validation, organization, reminders, and educational content—not legal advice or representation.
about.paragraph2: Our goal is to reduce confusion and errors that push people toward expensive services for straightforward paperwork. You stay in control; we provide the tools and general information to help you move through the process confidently.
about.paragraph3: We are not a law firm and do not negotiate with insurance companies on your behalf. When a situation is too complex for standard PIP filing alone, we point you toward professional resources—see our “When to Call an Attorney” page.

familyStory.title: Our Family Story
familyStory.subtitle: ClaimSaver+ was born from a personal experience that changed our family forever.

personalJourney.title: A Personal Journey
personalJourney.description: After our family faced the challenges of navigating the complex world of accident claims and administrative fees, we realized there had to be a better way.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: To give Florida families straightforward, affordable tools to file and manage no-fault claims—without paying contingency fees for paperwork they can complete with good guidance and the platform.

whyWeCare.title: Why We Care
whyWeCare.quote: "We created this platform to help families like ours avoid the financial burden of traditional administrative services during already difficult times."

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: To give Florida drivers affordable, transparent tools to file and manage no-fault claims—with honest clarity about what software can and can’t do.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: A trusted alternative for drivers who don’t need an attorney for a standard PIP claim—professional-grade organization and education when you choose to manage the filing directly.

values.title: Our Values
values.description: Transparency, honesty about what we can and cannot do, and respect for your autonomy—your claim, your decisions.

support.title: Customer Support
support.description: Our support team handles platform and billing questions. For legal questions about your specific situation, we’ll point you to the right resources—including a licensed attorney when that’s what you need.

coreValues.title: Our Core Values
coreValues.subtitle: The principles that guide everything we do at ClaimSaver+

process.title: A typical
process.subtitle: guided filing flow
process.description: You perform each step; the platform helps you stay organized

step1.title: Enter your information
step1.description: Use our guided forms to enter your accident details and upload documents into your secure account. We do not “analyze” your claim for legal merit—you provide the facts.

step2.title: Track your recovery
step2.description: Log medical appointments, upload treatment-related records you choose, and track expenses using the built-in tools. Follow your healthcare providers’ instructions—ClaimSaver+ does not give medical advice.

step3.title: Stay on track
step3.description: Use calendars and reminders for deadlines and follow-ups. Reminders reflect common timelines (like the 14-day initial treatment rule) as general information—not legal advice about what you must do.

step4.title: File your claim
step4.description: When you’re ready, compile your documents into a complete package and submit it to your insurer. Staying organized helps you file a complete claim—you communicate with your insurance company directly.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Get answers to the most common questions about our services

whatDoWeDo.question: What exactly does ClaimSaver+ do for me?
whatDoWeDo.answer: We provide software: guided forms, validation, secure storage, expense tracking, reminders, and education. You file your claim and communicate with your insurer. We do not negotiate, evaluate legal merit, or represent you.

documents.question: What documents do I need to provide?
documents.answer: You'll need to provide police reports, medical records, insurance information, photos of the accident scene, vehicle damage photos, medical bills, and any other evidence related to your case. Our platform makes it easy to upload and organize these documents securely.

security.question: How secure is my personal information?
security.answer: We use bank-level security with end-to-end encryption for all data. Our platform is HIPAA-compliant and follows strict security protocols to protect your personal information, medical records, and case documents. Your privacy and security are our top priorities.

mobile.question: Can I access my case information on my phone?
mobile.answer: Yes. The platform works in modern mobile browsers so you can upload documents and use your dashboard on the go.

help.question: What if I need help during the process?
help.answer: Contact us for platform, billing, or technical issues. We cannot tell you what you should do in your specific legal situation—that requires a licensed attorney. If your matter is too complex for standard PIP filing alone, see our “When to Call an Attorney” page.

timeline.question: How long does the entire process take?
timeline.answer: The timeline varies depending on the complexity of your case and the state where the accident occurred. Generally, the initial form filing happens within 24-48 hours. The overall recovery process typically takes 3-6 months, but we work to expedite your case as much as possible.

guarantee.question: Do you guarantee compensation?
guarantee.answer: No. ClaimSaver+ does not guarantee any outcome or settlement amount. We provide tools and general education only. Results depend on your facts, coverage, and how your insurer handles the claim.
